TestCaseName: Verify coverage rows sorted alphabetically on initial load
Description: Confirm the Sub Limits coverage rows load sorted alphabetically by Coverage Name on initial display.
Preconditions: User logged in; Energy Property submission with many coverages
Steps: 1. Login to EzDocs.\n2. Open the Energy Property submission.\n3. Navigate to Coverage → Sub Limits.
ExpectedResult: Coverage rows are displayed in alphabetical order (A→Z) by Coverage Name on initial load., Top/border header rows remain unchanged.

Description: Confirm alphabetical ordering is maintained when navigating between pages of the Sub Limits list.
Steps:
1. Open Coverage → Sub Limits.
2. Note coverage names on page 1.
3. Use top Next to go to page 2 and note first/last names.
4. Use Last to jump to final page and inspect ordering.
ExpectedResult: Each page displays its subset of coverages that continue the global alphabetical order; no page shows names out of global alphabetical sequence.

TestCaseName: Verify vertical scrollbar present in Sub Limits container
Description: Check that the Sub Limits container shows an internal vertical scrollbar when content overflows its height.
Preconditions: Submission with enough rows to overflow visible container
Steps: 1. Open Coverage → Sub Limits.\n2. Inspect the Sub Limits container for a vertical scrollbar.\n3. Visually confirm scrollbar thumb moves when scrolling.
ExpectedResult: A vertical scrollbar is present inside the Sub Limits area and the scroll thumb moves indicating internal scrolling capability.

TestCaseName: Verify inner scrolling does not move page header/footer
Description: Ensure scrolling inside the Sub Limits list only scrolls the list content, not the entire page.
Steps: 1. Focus mouse over Sub Limits container.\n2. Scroll using mouse wheel or touchpad.\n3. Observe whether page header/footer shift.
ExpectedResult: Only the Sub Limits container content scrolls; header and top coverage area remain fixed during inner scroll.
Comments: Include keyboard scroll (Arrow/PageDown) while list focused.

AcceptanceCriteria: Add Page move function at the top of page as well
TestCaseName: Verify top pagination controls exist and are visible
Description: Confirm that pagination controls (first, prev, next, last) appear at the top of the Sub Limits list and are visible on load.
Steps: 1. Open Coverage → Sub Limits.\n2. Look for pagination controls at the top of the list.
ExpectedResult: Top pagination controls (icons/buttons) are present, visible, and labelled with accessible titles/ARIA attributes.
Comments: Also verify visible on narrow widths.

AcceptanceCriteria: Add Page move function at the top of page as well
Description: Validate top pagination (Next/Prev/First/Last) navigates to corresponding pages and updates content and indicators.
Steps: 1. Use top Next to advance a page and verify content changes.\n2. Use top Last and verify last page content.\n3. Use top First to return to first page.
ExpectedResult: Top pagination controls navigate correctly and the list content updates to show appropriate records; page indicators update accordingly.
Comments: Record page numbers shown in indicator.

AcceptanceCriteria: Verify top and bottom page indicators
TestCaseName: Top and bottom indicators show identical ranges and totals
Description: Confirm both top and bottom pagination indicators display the same 'x to y of z' text and update together.
Steps: 1. Note top indicator text.\n2. Note bottom indicator text.\n3. Use top Next and compare both again.
ExpectedResult: Top and bottom indicators always show identical range and total count after any navigation action.

AcceptanceCriteria: Bottom controls functional after using top controls
TestCaseName: Bottom pagination works after top navigation
Description: Verify bottom controls remain functional and in sync after using top pagination controls to navigate.
Steps: 1. Use top Next to go to page 2.\n2. Use bottom Next to go to page 3.\n3. Observe content and indicators update accordingly.
ExpectedResult: Bottom controls function normally after top-based navigation; content and indicators remain synchronized between top and bottom.
Comments: Also test reverse sequence (bottom then top).

TestCaseName: Pagination controls are keyboard-focusable and have ARIA labels
Description: Ensure pagination controls (top and bottom) are reachable via keyboard and announceable to assistive tech.
Steps:
1. Tab to the pagination controls at top.
2. Use Enter/Space to activate Next/Prev.
3. Verify control has ARIA-label or title.
4. Repeat for bottom controls.
ExpectedResult: Pagination controls are reachable by keyboard and expose ARIA labels/titles for screen readers; activation via keyboard triggers navigation.
Comments: Include tests with screen-reader or accessibility audit tool.

TestCaseName: Sub Limits list and pagination remain usable on narrow viewports
Description: Confirm the coverage list, inner scrollbar, and top/bottom pagination remain visible and usable on narrower screen widths.
Preconditions: Multi-page submission; responsive viewport (mobile/tablet) or devtools emulation
Steps:
1. Resize viewport to narrow width (mobile).
2. Open Coverage → Sub Limits.
3. Verify inner scroll and top pagination controls are usable and visible.
4. Navigate pages using top controls.
ExpectedResult: On narrow viewports the Sub Limits list and top/bottom pagination remain usable and accessible; no controls overlap or become unreachable.
Comments: Document any truncation and required style adjustments.

TestCaseName: Verify alphabetical order after adding a new coverage
Description: Ensure adding a new coverage entry causes the list to remain alphabetically ordered and the new item appears in correct position.
Preconditions: User with edit permissions; ability to add coverage
Steps: 1. Add a new coverage with name starting with 'B' (or a value fitting between existing entries).\n2. Refresh or re-open Sub Limits list.
ExpectedResult: The newly added coverage appears in the correct alphabetical position; list remains globally alphabetized.
column12: verify API call returns sorted results.
